sidebar.noMatchingTerminals: No terminals match the current filter
sidebar.claimMainBrowser: Become the leading browser — terminals will auto-resize to fit this window. Other browsers will use CSS scaling instead.
sidebar.leadingBrowser: This browser is leading — terminals auto-resize to fit this window
terminal.makeReferenceScaleBrowser: Make this the reference scale browser
terminal.resizeToThisViewport: Resize this terminal to this viewport
sidebar.saveWorkTerminalsClose: Save your work - terminals will close
lens.request.selectManyToContinue: Select one or more of {count} options to continue.
lens.request.needsOneAnswer: The agent needs one answer to continue.
lens.request.needsManyAnswers: The agent needs {count} answers to continue.
lens.request.requiredBeforeContinue: {label} required before the turn can continue.
lens.request.question: Question
lens.status.connectedWaiting: Lens is connected to MidTerm and waiting for history content.
lens.issue.busyTerminalTurn.title: Terminal owns the live Codex turn
lens.issue.busyTerminalTurn.body: Lens cannot take over while Terminal still owns the active Codex turn. Finish or interrupt that turn in Terminal, then retry.
lens.issue.busyTerminalTurn.bodyReadonly: Lens is showing the last stable history while the terminal Codex turn is still running. Finish or interrupt that turn in Terminal, then retry live Lens attach.
lens.issue.missingResumeId.title: No resumable Codex thread is known yet
lens.issue.missingResumeId.body: MidTerm cannot determine a resumable Codex thread id for this session yet, so live Lens attach is unavailable. Use Terminal for the live lane, or retry later.
lens.issue.missingResumeId.bodyReadonly: Lens can still show canonical history, but MidTerm does not yet know a resumable Codex thread id for live handoff in this session. Keep using Terminal for the live lane, or retry after the thread identity becomes known.
lens.issue.shellRecoveryFailed.title: Terminal did not recover cleanly after handoff
lens.issue.shellRecoveryFailed.body: MidTerm stopped the foreground Codex process but the session did not settle back into a clean live lane. Retry Lens once the lane is stable again.
lens.issue.nativeRuntimeUnavailable.titl: This session cannot start a live Lens runtime yet
lens.issue.nativeRuntimeUnavailable.body: MidTerm could not start the native Lens runtime for this session. Retry after the session becomes native-runtime-capable.
lens.issue.readonlyHistory.title: Live Lens attach is unavailable right now
lens.issue.readonlyHistory.body: {detail} Lens is staying open on canonical history, so you can still inspect the last stable history while Terminal remains the live fallback.
lens.issue.startupFailed.title: Lens could not open
lens.activation.opening.detail: Lens pane opened. Preparing Lens runtime attach.
lens.activation.opening.body: MidTerm is opening the Lens conversation surface for this session.
lens.activation.attaching.body: Starting or reconnecting the backend-owned Lens runtime for this session.
lens.activation.waitingSnapshot.detail: Lens runtime accepted the attach request.
lens.activation.waitingSnapshot.body: Waiting for the first canonical Lens snapshot from MidTerm.
lens.activation.loadingEvents.detail: Lens snapshot is ready. Loading recent history events.
lens.activation.loadingEvents.body: Loading the canonical Lens event backlog for this session.
lens.activation.connectingStream.detail: Lens data is loaded. Connecting the live stream.
lens.activation.connectingStream.body: Opening the live Lens event stream so the history updates in real time.
lens.activation.ready.body: Realtime canonical Lens events are now flowing into the history.
lens.activation.historyRestored.body: MidTerm recovered canonical Lens history after the initial attach failed, so it is retrying the live attach automatically.
lens.activation.startupFailed.detail: Lens startup failed before the first stable snapshot became available.
lens.activation.retry.detail: MidTerm is retrying the live Lens attach for this session.
lens.activation.snapshotReady.body: MidTerm produced the first canonical Lens snapshot on retry {attempt}.
sessionLauncher.title: Start a New Session
sessionLauncher.codexDescription: Start a Lens-backed Codex session in a chosen folder.
sessionLauncher.claudeDescription: Start a Lens-backed Claude session in a chosen folder.
sessionLauncher.localTargetDescription: Create the session on the current MidTerm host.
sessionLauncher.remoteWorkingDirectoryPl: Optional path on the remote machine
sessionLauncher.remoteWorkingDirectoryHi: Remote launches accept a path, but directory browsing is only available for the current server.
sessionLauncher.remoteTerminalOnly: Remote launches currently support Terminal sessions only.
sessionLauncher.settingsUnavailable: Settings are not loaded yet. Try again in a moment.
sidebar.trustCertificateHint: Trust certificate on this or other devices
security.warning: No password set. Anyone on the network can access your terminals.
chat.title: Voice Chat
terminal.createHint: Press <kbd>Alt</kbd>+<kbd>T</kbd> to create a new session
settings.general.title: Updates / Info
settings.general.latestVersion: You are running the latest version
settings.general.serverHint: Server settings are configured via command line arguments:
settings.general.bindHint: Bind address (default: 0.0.0.0, use 127.0.0.1 for local only)
settings.general.ossHint: MidTerm uses the following open source components:
settings.hub.title: MidTerm Hub
settings.hub.apiKeyStoredPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current API key
settings.hub.passwordStoredPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current password
settings.hub.passwordHint: Used if the API key is missing or rejected.
settings.hub.removeMachineConfirm: Remove this machine from MidTerm Hub?
settings.appearance.title: Appearance
settings.appearance.backgroundHint: Uploads a PNG or JPG and stores it beside the MidTerm logs.
settings.behavior.title: Behaviour
settings.behavior.inputModeHint: Smart Input uses a text box instead of direct keyboard input. Both mode docks the input bar below the terminal.
settings.behavior.disableAutoMainBrowser: Prevents a different browser from taking over as main just because it was the only active tab for a while. Use the terminal badge to claim main browser explicitly when you want to resize from that device.
settings.behavior.keepAwake: Keep computer awake while sessions exist
settings.behavior.keepAwakeHint: Prevents sleep or standby while MidTerm has at least one live session. The display may still turn off. On Mac laptops, closing the lid still puts the computer to sleep.
settings.behavior.resumeModeHint: Quick Resume keeps hidden sessions lighter by catching them up with a smaller replay burst when you revisit them. Visible sessions remain live, and the heat indicator still reflects real terminal activity.
settings.behavior.installIosMessage: To install MidTerm on iPhone or iPad, open this page in Safari, tap Share, then tap Add to Home Screen.
settings.behavior.tmuxHint: Enables AI coding tools to manage panes via tmux commands. Disable if you SSH into remote hosts that run real tmux. Requires restart.
settings.agentUi.title: Agent UI
settings.agentUi.agentEnvInvalid: Environment variables must use KEY=value format
settings.security.title: Security
settings.security.terminalUserHint: When running as a system service, terminals spawn as this user instead of the service account.
settings.security.fingerprintHint: When connecting from a new device, click the padlock icon in your browser and compare the SHA-256 fingerprint with this one. Matching fingerprints confirm you're connecting to the real server.
settings.security.regenerateHint: Delete and recreate the HTTPS certificate. MidTerm will restart and browsers will need to re-trust the new certificate.
settings.security.regenerateConfirm: This will delete the current HTTPS certificate, generate a new one, and restart the server. All connected browsers will need to re-trust the new certificate. Continue?
settings.security.apiKeysHint: Create machine tokens for agents and automation. New keys are shown only once after creation.
settings.security.apiKeyCreatedHint: Store this API key now. It will not be shown again:
settings.security.apiKeyCopyFailed: Clipboard copy failed. Save it manually.
settings.security.apiKeyRevokeConfirm: Revoke API key "{name}"? Agents using it will lose access immediately.
settings.security.firewallHint: Allow other PCs on your network to reach the current MidTerm HTTPS port.
settings.security.firewallLoopbackHint: MidTerm is currently bound to localhost only, so a firewall rule will not make it reachable from other PCs.
settings.security.firewallAdminRequired: MidTerm needs Administrator rights to add or remove Windows firewall rules.
settings.security.firewallStatusActive: Rule active for the current server port
settings.security.firewallStatusOutOfDat: A MidTerm rule exists but does not match the current server configuration
settings.security.firewallStatusMissing: No MidTerm firewall rule is configured
settings.diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
settings.diagnostics.frontendLoggingHint: Use the browser console (<kbd>F12</kbd>) to control frontend logging. Errors and warnings always appear. Info and verbose output is per-module.
settings.diagnostics.loggingPersistHint: Settings persist in localStorage across page reloads.
settings.diagnostics.restartServerDesc: Restart the server process. All clients will temporarily disconnect.
settings.diagnostics.restartConfirm: Are you sure you want to restart the server? All connected clients will be temporarily disconnected.
settings.diagnostics.restartFailed: Server may not have restarted. Try refreshing the page.
fileViewer.downloadHint: Use the download button to save this file.
git.title: Git
git.notARepoHint: Navigate to a git repository to see status
commands.title: Commands
session.splitTooltip: Session is in a split layout
session.injectGuidancePrompt.default: You are running inside MidTerm, a web terminal multiplexer. MidTerm keeps .midterm/ fresh in this working directory. This terminal can use MidTerm browser-control and split-pane helpers. Read .midterm/AGENTS.md now. MidTerm CLI helpers are available in .midterm/mtcli.sh and .midterm/mtcli.ps1.
session.injectGuidancePrompt.claude: You are running inside MidTerm, a web terminal multiplexer. MidTerm keeps .midterm/ fresh in this working directory. This terminal can use MidTerm browser-control, split-pane, and DOM-inspection helpers. Read .midterm/CLAUDE.md now. MidTerm CLI helpers are available in .midterm/mtcli.sh and .midterm/mtcli.ps1.
network.enableClipboard: Click to enable clipboard & secure features
fileDrop.browserBlocks: Your browser blocks clipboard access on untrusted connections.
managerBar.queue.chainCooldown: Next chain step waits for cooldown
managerBar.modal.title: Setup Middle Manager Action
managerBar.modal.intro: Build a reusable action for the active terminal session.
managerBar.modal.label: Label
managerBar.modal.labelHelp: Leave this blank to use the first non-empty prompt line as the button label.
managerBar.modal.cooldownHint: Starts when the session heat drops below 25%. After each trigger, heat is ignored for 5 seconds.
managerBar.modal.chainHint: Follow-up prompts wait for cooldown automatically.
managerBar.modal.promptSectionSingleCopy: Write the exact prompt MidTerm should send to the active session.
managerBar.modal.promptSectionChainCopy: Add the prompts in order. MidTerm waits for cooldown before sending each follow-up step.
managerBar.modal.behaviorSectionCopy: Choose how many prompts belong to this action and when the full action should start.
managerBar.modal.typeSingleDescription: One prompt is sent as written. Use this for one-shot reusable instructions.
managerBar.modal.typeChainDescription: A sequence of prompts sent in order. Each follow-up waits for cooldown automatically.
managerBar.modal.triggerDetailsCopy: Only the controls that matter for the selected trigger are shown here.
managerBar.modal.triggerDescription.fire: Runs immediately once when you tap the action.
managerBar.modal.triggerDescription.onCo: Queues once and waits until the active session cools down below 25%.
managerBar.modal.triggerDescription.repe: Runs the full action repeatedly until the repeat count is reached.
Queues the full action on a fixed interval.
managerBar.modal.triggerDescription.sche: Queues the full action at the next matching scheduled time.
managerBar.modal.repeatCountHelp: The full action runs again after it finishes and the session cools down.
managerBar.modal.repeatIntervalHelp: The full action is queued on this cadence. Chain steps still wait for cooldown.
managerBar.modal.scheduleHelp: Add one or more start times. MidTerm will queue the full action at the next matching slot.
managerBar.modal.errorPromptRequired: Add at least one prompt before saving.
managerBar.modal.errorScheduleRequired: Add at least one schedule entry before saving.
agentView.laneMeta: What MidTerm can safely attach right now
agentView.noRecentOutput: No recent output in the terminal buffer.
agentView.transportCodex: This panel is backed by MidTerm's structured Lens runtime for Codex sessions and should render canonical turns, tools, requests, and streaming output.
agentView.transportClaude: This panel is backed by MidTerm's structured Lens runtime for Claude sessions and should render canonical turns, tools, requests, and streaming output.
agentView.transportFallback: Lens should render structured provider runtime state here. If that state is unavailable, treat it as a Lens runtime problem rather than a terminal-derived fallback.
agentView.sessionUnavailable: This session is no longer available.
agentView.loadError: Agent data could not be loaded right now.
webPreview.title: Web Preview
webPreview.detachedPlaceholder: Preview is open in a separate window
update.refreshRequiredNote: This tab needs the new UI to stay compatible.
update.refreshWhenConvenient: Refresh when convenient. Terminals stay connected.
auth.certVerifyHint: Verify this matches across devices. See Settings → Security for details.
share.copiedToClipboard: Connection info has been copied to your clipboard!
share.visitTrustPage: Visit the trust page to share access
share.dialog.title: Share Terminal Session
share.dialog.hint: The link grants access to this terminal session for 1 hour.
share.dialog.runningSharesUnavailable: Running shares could not be loaded right now.
share.shared.invalid: This shared session link is invalid or has expired.
trust.title: Trust This Server
trust.subtitle: MidTerm Terminal Multiplexer
trust.securityCheck: Compare this fingerprint with the one shown during installation or on your original trusted device. If they match, this connection is authentic.
trust.verifyBrowserHint: After installing, verify the connection is secure:
trust.securityWarning: Never enter passwords or sensitive information until you have verified the fingerprint matches. If your browser shows a certificate warning after previously working, re-verify the fingerprint before proceeding.
error.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 4 characters
smartInput.autoSendOnHint: Auto-send is enabled for transcribed input
smartInput.sendGestureHint: Send. Double-click or long-press to toggle auto-send
